name: Mother's Squash Pie
recipeInstructions:
Because of its Thanksgiving associations, pumpkin has become the pre-eminent squash for pie-making. Many 19th century connoisseurs, however, insisted that other winter squashes had a more refined flavor. This recipe (Mabel Gray's from "Yankee Magazine's Great New England Recipes," 1983) would also work with acorn and other squashes.
1. Heat milk and squash together in double boiler.
2. In a bowl, mix sugar, flour, salt, ginger, nutmeg, and cinnamon.
3. Add eggs to the dry mixture and beat well with a rotary beater.
4. Gradually add the egg mixture to the milk and squash in the double boiler, stirring until combined.
5. Do not allow the filling to boil.
6. Pour the warm filling into the pie shell.
7. Bake at 400°F for 10 minutes, then reduce the heat to 350°F and bake until the pie sets, about 15-20 minutes.
Makes 6-8 servings.
Each serving contains approximately: 428 calories; 349 milligrams sodium; 109 milligrams cholesterol; 21 grams fat; 54 grams carbohydrates; 8 grams protein; 0.53 grams fiber.
